questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of monera?
questions.explanation: Monera is a kingdom that includes prokaryotic organisms, which reproduce by binary fission.

questions.q: What is the process called when an organism becomes reproductively isolated from its original population?
questions.explanation: Speciation is the process by which a new species emerges from an existing one.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of homologous structures?
opts: (D) Leaves of plants and roots of plants
questions.explanation: Forelimbs of humans and birds are homologous structures, as they have a common ancestor and similar bone structure.

questions.q: What is the term for the process by which an organism becomes better suited to its environment?
questions.explanation: Adaptation is the process by which an organism becomes better suited to its environment over time.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of convergent evolution?
opts: (D) Leaves of plants and roots of plants
questions.explanation: Wings of butterflies and birds are examples of convergent evolution, as they have evolved similar structures independently to adapt to their environments.

questions.q: What is the term for the study of the diversity of life on Earth?
questions.explanation: Biodiversity is the study of the diversity of life on Earth, including the variety of species, ecosystems, and genetic variation.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of prokaryotic cells?
questions.explanation: Prokaryotic cells lack a true nucleus and other membrane-bound organelles.

questions.q: What is the process by which an organism becomes reproductively isolated from its original population due to geographical barriers?
questions.explanation: Speciation can occur due to geographical barriers, resulting in the formation of new species.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of an autotrophic organism?
questions.explanation: Plants are autotrophic organisms, as they produce their own food through photosynthesis.

questions.q: What is the term for the study of the structure and function of cells?
questions.explanation: Cell biology is the study of the structure and function of cells.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of eukaryotic cells?
questions.explanation: Eukaryotic cells typically have a true nucleus, which is membrane-bound.

questions.q: What is the process by which an organism becomes better suited to its environment through genetic variation?
questions.explanation: Adaptation can occur through genetic variation, resulting in the formation of new traits.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a heterotrophic organism?
questions.explanation: Animals are heterotrophic organisms, as they cannot produce their own food and must consume other organisms or plants.

questions.q: What is the term for the study of the genetic variation within a population?
questions.explanation: Population genetics is the study of the genetic variation within a population.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of symbiotic relationships?
opts: (A) One organism benefits, the other is harmed
(C) One organism benefits, the other is not affected
questions.explanation: Symbiotic relationships can be mutualistic, where both organisms benefit.

questions.q: What is the process by which an organism becomes reproductively isolated from its original population due to changes in behavior?
questions.explanation: Speciation can occur due to changes in behavior, resulting in the formation of new species.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of an endosymbiotic relationship?
opts: (A) Symbiotic relationship between a plant and a fungus
(B) Symbiotic relationship between a coral and a fish
(C) Symbiotic relationship between mitochondria and a cell
(D) Symbiotic relationship between a parasite and a host
questions.explanation: Mitochondria are an example of endosymbiotic relationships, where they originated from bacteria that lived within ancient cells.

questions.q: What is the term for the study of the distribution and abundance of organisms in different environments?
questions.explanation: Ecology is the study of the distribution and abundance of organisms in different environments.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of co-evolution?
opts: (A) One organism benefits, the other is harmed
(C) One organism benefits, the other is not affected
questions.explanation: Co-evolution occurs when two or more species evolve together, often resulting in mutualistic relationships.

questions.q: What is the process by which an organism becomes reproductively isolated from its original population due to changes in the environment?
questions.explanation: Speciation can occur due to changes in the environment, resulting in the formation of new species.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a holobiont?
opts: (C) A symbiotic relationship between a coral and a fish
(D) A community of organisms and their associated microorganisms
questions.explanation: A holobiont is a community of organisms and their associated microorganisms, such as a coral and its associated algae.
